data.title: Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission: The public should beware of the unlicensed trading platform "BiyaPay"
data.description: The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) issued a warning today, alerting the public to an unlicensed trading platform called "BiyaPay." The platform claims to offer Hong Kong and US stock trading services and maintains an office in Hong Kong, but has never been licensed by the SFC or applied for any other license. Under Hong Kong law, operating securities trading without a license or promoting such services to Hong Kong investors is illegal. The SFC discovered "BiyaPay" through its social media monitoring platform and noted that members of the public in Hong Kong can access platform information and register accounts through its official website and social media accounts. The SFC has now placed the platform on its warning list.

data.description: XPL has been listed on Binance Alpha and is currently trading at 1.309 USDT.

data.title: Fed Governor Milan: I think interest rates should be gradually reduced by 50 basis points
data.description: Fed Governor Milan: I think interest rates are too restrictive, ranging from 150 to 200 basis points, and should be gradually reduced in 50 basis point increments. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Weekly Jobless Claims Fall Below Expectations
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the number of initial jobless claims in the United States for the week ending September 20 was reported at 218,000. This figure is lower than the anticipated 235,000 claims. The previous week's data was revised from 231,000 to 232,000.

data.title: Blockchain-Based Lending Firm Divine Secures $6.6 Million in Seed Funding
data.description: According to PANews, blockchain-based micro-lending company Divine has successfully raised $6.6 million in a seed funding round, with Paradigm leading the investment. The funding aims to enhance Divine's capabilities in providing innovative financial solutions through blockchain technology.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Goolsbee Observes Cooling Job Market and Rising Inflation
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Federal Reserve official Austan Goolsbee noted that the job market appears to be cooling while inflation is on the rise. These observations come amid ongoing economic assessments by the Federal Reserve to address the current economic conditions.

data.title: Ransomware group Rhysida auctions Maryland Department of Transportation data for 30 BTC
data.description: Bitcoin News published an article on the X platform stating that the ransomware organization Rhysida stole sensitive data from the Maryland Department of Transportation and auctioned it for 30 BTC (about US$3.4 million).

data.title: Binance to List Plasma (XPL) Across Multiple Trading Services
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, Plasma (XPL) is set to be integrated into several Binance services, including Simple Earn, Buy Crypto, Binance Convert, Binance Margin, and Binance Futures. The rollout will occur at specific dates and times, beginning with the listing of XPL Flexible Products on Binance Simple Earn on 2025-09-25 at 13:00 (UTC). A special offer featuring a 200% APR for XPL Locked Products will be available starting 2025-09-26 at 00:00 (UTC) for eligible users. This offer is accessible on a first-come, first-served basis, with specific subscription limits in place.

To qualify for the special offer, new Binance users must complete identity verification during the promotion period, while existing verified users need to achieve a minimum spot trading volume of $50 equivalent. The promotion runs from 2025-09-26 00:00 (UTC) to 2025-10-09 23:59 (UTC). Users can expect up to a 48-hour delay before accessing the offer. Additionally, XPL will be available for purchase using various payment methods, including VISA, MasterCard, Google Pay, and Apple Pay, within an hour of its listing on Binance Spot.

Binance Convert will allow trading of XPL against BTC, USDT, and other tokens at zero fees shortly after its spot listing. On the margin front, XPL will be added as a borrowable asset on Cross and Isolated Margin, with trading pairs XPL/USDT and XPL/USDC available from 2025-09-25 at 13:00 (UTC). Users are advised to exercise caution due to potential volatility of newly listed tokens. Binance Futures will transition the USDⓈ-M XPL Perpetual Contract from pre-market trading to standard trading on 2025-09-25 at 13:00 (UTC), with a gradual transition period lasting up to three hours. The contract will support multi-assets mode, allowing trading across various margin assets. Further adjustments to contract specifications may occur based on market conditions.

data.title: Binance: XPL airdrops will resume at 19:45 (UTC+8)
data.description: Binance announced that the Plasma (XPL) airdrop will resume at 19:45 (UTC+8).

data.title: U.S. Economic Indicators Show Slight Increase in Core PCE and GDP
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the final annualized quarterly rate for the U.S. core Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index in the second quarter was 2.6%, slightly above the expected 2.5% and the previous value of 2.50%.

Additionally, the final annualized quarterly rate for the U.S. real GDP in the second quarter was reported at 3.8%, surpassing the anticipated 3.30% and matching the prior figure of 3.30%.

data.title: OKX Wallet has fixed the issue of incomplete balance display
data.description: OKX announced in a statement that it has fixed the issue where the Wallet displayed incomplete asset balances.

data.title: White House Considers Candidates for CFTC Chairmanship
data.description:
According to Foresight News, the White House is evaluating potential candidates for the position of Chairman of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). The contenders include former CFTC Commissioner Jill Sommers and National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) Chairman Kyle Hauptman. Both individuals are perceived as supportive of cryptocurrency.

Jill Sommers has a decade-long tenure at Patomak Global Partners, a consultancy founded by former U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Commissioner Paul Atkins, with whom she shares a close professional relationship. Meanwhile, Kyle Hauptman, the 13th Chairman of the NCUA, previously held the role of Senior Vice President at investment bank Jefferies, where he oversaw U.S. Treasury and futures trading.

The decision to appoint a new CFTC Chairman comes amid growing interest and regulatory scrutiny in the cryptocurrency sector. The White House's consideration of candidates with favorable views on digital currencies signals a potential shift in regulatory approach towards the industry.

data.title: Federal Reserve Faces Economic Risks Without Swift Rate Cuts, Says Official
data.description:
According to PANews, Federal Reserve Governor Milan has expressed concerns that the U.S. economy could face risks if interest rates are not reduced promptly. He highlighted that the current policy rate, ranging from 4% to 4.25%, is highly restrictive and significantly above what he considers the 'neutral' level. Milan emphasized the importance of adjusting rates sooner rather than later, stating, "When monetary policy is in a restrictive stance, the economy is more susceptible to downside shocks. In my view, there is really no need to take this risk."

Milan suggested that the Federal Reserve could implement consecutive rate cuts of 50 basis points in a short period, followed by a cautious approach once the target is achieved. This strategy aims to realign monetary policy without exposing the economy to unnecessary risks.

data.title: Metis founder Elena published an open letter to the community, outlining the strategic evolution path centered on AI and playability.
data.description: Elena Sinelnikova, co-founder of Metis and advisor to LazAI, published an open letter to the community, systematically outlining Metis' strategic plan to evolve from "Metis beyond Layer 2" to "a testing ground for the next generation of groundbreaking decentralized applications." Metis' infrastructure stack is clearly divided into: Andromeda as the settlement layer, and Hyperion as the AI-optimized high-performance computing layer. Together, Andromeda and Hyperion form the infrastructure. LazAI, on the other hand, serves as the application and data layer focused on "playability." Metis believes that traditional Layer 2 requires an upgrade. Infrastructure alone is insufficient to attract mass adoption; playability and meaningful interactions must be enhanced. LazAI and its first AI companion data asset (DAT), Lazbubu, are key implementations of this strategy. Data shows that Lazbubu's whitelisting campaign has attracted over 90,000 users and minted over 10,000 tokens, initially validating the market demand for the "AI agent as asset" model. In her letter, Elena called on the community to participate in the development of new-layer ecosystems like LazAI and to jointly shape the future. This move marks that Metis is shifting its strategic focus from a single scaling solution to building a comprehensive ecosystem that integrates blockchain, AI and community-driven development.

data.title: Global Crypto Adoption Index Highlights Regulatory Impact on Usage
data.description:
Binance Blog published a new article, revealing insights into a recent trend observed in the Chainalysis Global Crypto Adoption Index. The article highlights how jurisdictions with clearer and proportionate digital-asset regulations are experiencing stronger grassroots crypto usage. The United States and Brazil serve as prominent examples, while South Korea's new user-protection law and the UK's recent policy momentum indicate similar trends. The data suggests that when governments establish reasonable, risk-based rules for crypto usage, adoption increases significantly. The Chainalysis Index illustrates this relationship, showing that countries clarifying digital asset integration into their financial systems in 2024 and 2025 have climbed the rankings due to real activity rather than sentiment alone.

The Chainalysis Global Crypto Adoption Index ranks countries based on on- and off-chain signals related to user and institutional engagement with crypto. It comprises four sub-indices: on-chain value received by centralized services (adjusted for purchasing power), retail-sized value received at centralized services, on-chain value received by DeFi protocols, and institutional-sized value received at centralized services. This ranking captures actual crypto usage at the wallet and venue level, rather than mere discussions. The United States exemplifies how regulation and adoption reinforce each other. In 2024, the country ranked fourth on the Index, with spot bitcoin exchange-traded funds providing a compliant channel for mainstream participation. By 2025, the U.S. rose to second place, reflecting deeper institutional allocation and broader retail engagement. Key developments included approvals of spot bitcoin ETFs, bipartisan progress on a federal stablecoin framework, and congressional debate on broader market-structure legislation.

Brazil's approach to virtual-assets regulation has also fostered grassroots momentum. The country passed a foundational virtual-assets law in late 2022, enforced it in 2023, and advanced detailed licensing and supervisory standards in 2024. This constructive approach coincided with a steady climb in adoption, placing Brazil fifth in the 2025 Index. The domestic context, including Pix, the national instant-payments system integrated with Binance Pay, has accustomed consumers and merchants to fast digital money flows. As of 2025, Pix serves over 165 million people, processing more than 63 billion transactions in 2024 alone. With clearer rules for virtual-asset service providers and high-penetration digital rails, Brazilians are increasingly using crypto for saving, investing, and transferring value.

South Korea's trajectory further supports the notion that user protection catalyzes adoption. The Virtual Asset User Protection Act, effective this year, tightened standards on custody, market abuse, disclosures, and insurance while clarifying service provider obligations. Consequently, Korea improved from nineteenth to fifteenth in the rankings between 2024 and 2025. The Act ensured participation remained onshore and aligned with supervisory expectations, enhancing safety and sustainability. Institutions and retail users now have a clearer understanding of best practices, benefiting digital-asset venues meeting those standards. Meanwhile, regulatory momentum is becoming coordinated globally. The UK-US Transatlantic Taskforce for Markets of the Future prioritizes digital assets, setting a 180-day timeline for concrete proposals. The group focuses on stablecoins and tokenization, including supervisory sandboxes and cross-border interoperability.

Grassroots adoption benefits both retail and institutional users. Clear, predictable rules reduce compliance risk for institutions and provide a stable basis for investment in custody, market integrity, and consumer protection. They also offer macro-level benefits, such as more efficient cross-border payments, deeper capital formation, and a healthier mix of savings vehicles for households and small businesses. Policymakers are realizing that enforcement alone does not build safer markets—clear standards do. Countries implementing concrete licensing regimes, reporting requirements, and consumer protections are climbing the ladder. Binance supports harmonized, risk-based regulation, as data shows it works. The company aims to turn policy clarity into practical participation, providing global liquidity across spot and derivatives, institutional execution, and OTC blocks for low-impact rebalancing, along with custody and compliance frameworks built for rigorous scrutiny.

The Chainalysis Global Crypto Adoption Index measures actual crypto usage by people and institutions. Across two consecutive years, the pattern is clear: when regulation becomes transparent and workable, adoption grows broader and more resilient. The United States and Brazil illustrate this most clearly, South Korea's new user-protection regime is pushing in the same direction, and the UK-US taskforce hints at a new phase of cross-border coordination. This is how the next chapter will be written: clear rules, real users, and better infrastructure connecting them. Industry actors like Binance have the responsibility to operationalize these frameworks in ways that promote resilience, transparency, and user protection. As rules converge, the opportunity emerges to build deeper, safer usage of digital-asset infrastructure.

data.title: Bitcoin mining company Cipher Mining plans to issue $800 million in convertible senior notes in a private placement.
data.description: Bitcoin mining company Cipher Mining (NASDAQ: CIFR) plans to issue $800 million in aggregate principal amount of 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2031 in a private placement to qualified institutional buyers. Cipher also expects to grant the initial purchasers of the notes a 13-day option to purchase up to an additional $120 million in principal amount of the notes. The notes will mature on October 1, 2031, unless earlier redeemed, repurchased, or converted. Cipher intends to use a portion of the net proceeds from the offering to pay the cost of the call option transactions and the remaining proceeds to fund the company's data center construction in Barber Lake, accelerate the full deployment of its 2.4 GW high-performance computing (HPC) strategy, continue to expand its development site pipeline, and for general corporate purposes. (Globe newswire)

data.title: Google Secures Stake in Cipher Mining with $1.4 Billion Contract Guarantee
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Google has acquired the right to purchase a 5.4% stake in cryptocurrency mining company Cipher Mining Inc. Cipher announced that Google agreed to guarantee $1.4 billion in obligations under a contract signed between Fluidstack and Cipher. The initial 10-year agreement between Fluidstack and Cipher involves leasing data center capacity, generating approximately $3 billion in revenue. Cipher's data centers have previously been used for cryptocurrency mining. The company plans to use Google's support agreement to secure funding for expansion. The contract includes two potential five-year extensions, which could add an additional $4 billion in revenue. Cipher's stock surged by 24% in pre-market trading on the New York Stock Exchange, closing at $14.14 on Wednesday, more than doubling its value this year.

This deal is similar to Google's agreement with another cryptocurrency mining data center operator, TeraWulf Inc., where Google obtained warrants to acquire up to 14% of the company. In that transaction, Google also agreed to support a ten-year Fluidstack contract to purchase its capacity.
